frameworks.name: prospect theory
frameworks.one_line: reference-dependent value with concave gains, convex losses, tested across risky/riskless domains

frameworks.name: expected utility theory
frameworks.one_line: domain-general utility over outcomes without gain-loss reference dependence

core_question: Do gain-loss reflection effects predicted by prospect theory generalize from risky to riskless choice within the same subjects?
key_assumptions: prospect theory predicts symmetric reflection effects across risky and riskless domains
reference-dependence operates via concave gains and convex losses
within-subject design isolates domain-specific deviations from individual preference heterogeneity
manipulations: domain of choice (risky lottery vs. riskless)
outcome valence (gains vs. losses around a reference point)
key_findings: gain-loss reflection effects are observed in risky choice as predicted
the same subjects show no reflection effect under riskless choice
reference-dependence pattern is not consistent across domains within individuals
interpretations: a single reference-dependent utility cannot jointly describe risky and riskless choice
applying prospect-theoretic gain-loss curvature to riskless decisions is empirically unsupported
